title: Crispy Rose Middle Eastern Almond Wrap
description: A floral, crispy, energizing wrap that blends almond flour flatbread with spiced lamb, toasted almonds, and a hint of rose water for a vibrant Middle Eastern flavor.
instructions: In a bowl combine almond flour, baking powder, water, egg, and a pinch of salt to form a sticky dough.

Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and lightly coat with olive oil. Drop spoonfuls of dough to make 4 thin pancakes, cooking until bubbles form (2‑3 minutes), then flip and cook another 1‑2 minutes until golden.
Meanwhile, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium. Add ground lamb, season with salt, pepper, cumin, and chili flakes, and cook until browned, breaking up the meat (about 5‑6 minutes).
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the lamb, cooking until softened (2 minutes). Remove from heat, stir in toasted almonds and a splash of rose water, then let cool slightly.
Place a pancake on a clean surface, spoon 2‑3 tablespoons of lamb mixture in the center, and fold the edges over to form a tight wrap.
For extra crispness, return the wrap to the skillet, press gently with a spatula, and cook 1‑2 minutes on each side until edges are golden.
Drizzle a small amount of rose water over the finished wrap to enhance the floral aroma.
Serve immediately as a quick, energizing lunch or snack.
